title: Add Face Detection (Webcam + Photo Upload) to shinchan diary app

requirements.text: Add a new authenticated Face Detector page that supports both (1) live webcam face detection and (2) photo upload face detection, running entirely in the browser (no server-side processing).
acceptanceCriteria: A new route exists for the face detector page and it is wrapped in the existing <RequireAuth> gate, consistent with other diary features.
The page provides two clear modes (e.g., tabs or segmented controls) labeled in English: “Webcam” and “Photo Upload”.
Webcam mode: user can start and stop the camera; when running, the UI renders the video and overlays detected face bounding boxes in real time (or near-real time).
Photo Upload mode: user can select an image file; the UI renders the image and overlays detected face bounding boxes; user can clear and upload another image.
If camera permission is denied or no camera is available, show a clear English error message and the page remains usable (photo upload still works).
All detection is performed locally in the browser; no images/video are uploaded to the backend.

requirements.text: Integrate a browser-based face detection library (e.g., face-api.js) and add required model assets as static files so the Face Detector page works in production builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend dependencies required for face detection are added and build successfully.
Required face detection model files are stored under frontend/public (or an equivalent static path) and are fetched by the browser at runtime without any backend image/model routing.
The app shows a visible loading state while face detection models are being downloaded/initialized, and disables detection controls until initialization completes.
If model loading fails (e.g., network error), the UI shows a clear English error message with a suggested retry action.

requirements.text: Add an entry point to reach the Face Detector page from the existing app UI (navigation) using English user-facing labels.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a visible navigation element (e.g., header link/button or entries page action) labeled “Face Detector” that routes to the Face Detector page.
Navigation uses the existing TanStack Router setup (frontend/src/App.tsx) and does not break existing routes (/ , /entries, /entries/new, /entries/$entryId).

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/immutablePaths (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui). Compose or add new files instead.
Keep the backend as a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo). Face detection must be implemented on the frontend only; no backend endpoints or storage for images/video.
Use English for any user-facing text added by this change.
nonGoals: Storing face detection results, images, or video in the backend canister.
User identity verification, face recognition (who the person is), or any biometric authentication flow.
Real-time multi-user features (WebSockets/chat) related to face detection.
